hard.definition: A feeling of pensive sadness with no obvious cause.
hard.example: A sense of melancholy filled the empty room.

hard.definition: Lasting for a very short time.
hard.example: The ephemeral beauty of cherry blossoms delighted everyone.

hard.definition: Holding firmly to a position, principle, or course of action.
hard.example: Her tenacious spirit helped her succeed.

hard.definition: A complicated irregular network of passages or paths.
hard.example: They got lost in the labyrinth of streets.

hard.definition: Attractive and impressive through being richly colorful or sumptuous.
hard.example: She looked resplendent in her golden gown.

hard.definition: Not aware of or concerned about what is happening around one.
hard.example: He was oblivious to the noise around him.

hard.definition: A harsh discordant mixture of sounds.
hard.example: The cacophony of the city overwhelmed her senses.

hard.definition: Continue in a course of action despite difficulty or delay.
hard.example: You must persevere through the hardest challenges.

hard.definition: A group of many islands clustered together in a sea.
hard.example: The archipelago stretched across the ocean.

hard.definition: Open to more than one interpretation; not having one obvious meaning.
hard.example: The contract had several ambiguous clauses.

hard.definition: Well meaning and kindly; generous in giving to those in need.
hard.example: The benevolent leader donated to schools.

hard.definition: Inspiring fear or respect through being impressively large or capable.
hard.example: The opponent was a formidable challenger.

hard.definition: Fond of company; sociable.
hard.example: Her gregarious personality made her popular.

hard.definition: A system in which members are ranked according to status or authority.
hard.example: She rose to the top of the corporate hierarchy.

hard.definition: Certain to happen; unavoidable.
hard.example: Defeat seemed inevitable after losing their best player.

hard.definition: Place or deal with close together for contrasting effect.
hard.example: The painting juxtaposes light and dark perfectly.

hard.definition: Showing great attention to detail or correct behavior.
hard.example: She was meticulous in her research.

hard.definition: Famous or well known, typically for some bad quality or deed.
hard.example: He was notorious for arriving late.

hard.definition: Present everywhere at the same time.
hard.example: Technology has become omnipresent in modern life.

hard.definition: Not securely held or in position; dangerously likely to fall.
hard.example: The climber clung to a precarious ledge.

hard.definition: Representing the most perfect example of a quality or class.
hard.example: She was the quintessential professional athlete.

hard.definition: Examined or inspected closely and thoroughly.
hard.example: Every detail of the plan was scrutinized.

hard.definition: Provide lodging or sufficient space for; fit in with the needs of.
hard.example: The hotel can accommodate up to 500 guests.

hard.definition: Accept something reluctantly but without protest.
hard.example: She acquiesced to the demands despite her reservations.

hard.definition: A system of government with many complicated rules and processes.
hard.example: The bureaucracy slowed the approval process considerably.

hard.definition: Wishing to do what is right, especially to do one's work well.
hard.example: She was a conscientious worker who never cut corners.

hard.definition: Remove moisture from; cause to become completely dry.
hard.example: The desert heat would desiccate anything left in the sun.

hard.definition: A period of one thousand years.
hard.example: The new millennium brought great technological advances.

hard.definition: A pattern or device that aids memory.
hard.example: He used a mnemonic to remember the planets' order.

hard.definition: Consisting of items or parts of different kinds and qualities.
hard.example: The box held miscellaneous items from the old attic.

hard.definition: An officer in the armed forces ranking below a captain.
hard.example: The lieutenant gave orders to her squad.

hard.definition: An incident or event, especially one that is unexpected.
hard.example: Rain in the desert is a rare occurrence.

hard.definition: Lung infection causing inflammation of the air sacs.
hard.example: He was hospitalized with a severe case of pneumonia.

hard.definition: Take the place of; cause to be set aside or replaced.
hard.example: Digital cameras superseded film cameras in the 2000s.

hard.definition: Close observation, especially of a suspected person or group.
hard.example: The bank had extensive surveillance cameras installed.

hard.definition: The formation of a word from the sound it describes.
hard.example: Words like buzz and hiss are examples of onomatopoeia.

hard.definition: An act of speaking one's thoughts aloud regardless of any hearers.
hard.example: Hamlet's famous soliloquy begins with 'To be or not to be'.

hard.definition: A mode of behavior or thought peculiar to an individual.
hard.example: His habit of humming while working was a quirky idiosyncrasy.

hard.definition: A tube producing changing symmetrical patterns, or a vivid, complex sequence.
hard.example: The festival was a kaleidoscope of color and sound.

hard.definition: Miscellaneous articles or equipment needed for a particular activity.
hard.example: She arrived with all the paraphernalia of a professional chef.

hard.definition: Treating serious issues with deliberately inappropriate humor.
hard.example: His facetious comment lightened the tense meeting.

hard.definition: Feeling or appearing casually calm and relaxed; not displaying anxiety.
hard.example: She remained nonchalant despite the chaos around her.

hard.definition: A renewal of interest or activity; a revival.
hard.example: The city experienced a renaissance of art and culture.

hard.definition: Mutual trust and friendship among people who spend time together.
hard.example: The team was known for its extraordinary camaraderie.

hard.definition: An event causing great suffering, destruction, and distress.
hard.example: The oil spill was an environmental catastrophe.

hard.definition: Make someone feel very happy, animated, or elated.
hard.example: The mountain view never failed to exhilarate her.

hard.definition: Committing or involving a violation of what is regarded as sacred.
hard.example: Many considered the renovation of the chapel sacrilegious.

hard.definition: Kept secret in order to avoid being noticed.

hard.definition: The occurrence and development of events by pleasant chance.
hard.example: Finding that book was pure serendipity.

hard.definition: Having a ready insight into things; shrewd.
hard.example: The perspicacious detective solved the case in an hour.

hard.definition: Having an obstinately uncooperative attitude toward authority.
hard.example: The recalcitrant student refused to follow any rules.

hard.definition: The day of the week between Tuesday and Thursday.
hard.example: The meeting is scheduled for Wednesday afternoon.
